examples: Heavy commitment by an executive to a course of action, especially if it has worked well in the past, makes it likely to miss signs of incipient trouble or misinterpret them when they do appear.
My biggest problem is that people always misinterpret me as being aggressive.
With a bit of creativity, you can deliberately misinterpret pretty much anything she says.
He chose Eric the Legal Beagle, clever enough to misinterpret anything to the way Barry wanted.
On the other hand, if he did deliberately misinterpret his data, that is an offence.
